sections.label: Section 4.558
sections.original: καλεῖ δ ἀκούοντας οὐδὲν ἐν [*] μέσᾳ
sections.translation: He calls on those who hear nothing and he struggles in the midst of the whirling waters .

sections.label: Section 4.560
sections.original: γελᾷ δὲ δαίμων ἐπ ἀνδρὶ θερμῷ , [*]
sections.translation: The god laughs at the hot-headed man , seeing him , who boasted that this would never happen , exhausted by distress without remedy and unable to surmount the cresting wave . He wrecks the happiness of his earlier life on the reef of Justice ,

sections.label: Section 4.565
sections.original: ὤλετ ἄκλαυτος , ᾆστος . [*]
sections.translation: and he perishes unwept , unseen .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: κήρυσσε , κῆρυξ , καὶ στρατὸν κατειργαθοῦ ,
sections.translation: Herald , give the signal and restrain the crowd ; and let the piercing Tyrrhenian [*] trumpet , filled with human breath , send forth its shrill blare to the people !

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: πληρουμένου γὰρ τοῦδε βουλευτηρίου
sections.translation: For while this council-hall is filling , it is good to be silent , and for my ordinances to be learned , by the whole city for everlasting time , and by these appellants , so that their case may be decided well .

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: ἄναξ Ἄπολλον , ὧν ἔχεις αὐτὸς κράτει .
sections.translation: Lord Apollo , be master of what is yours .

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: τί τοῦδε σοὶ μέτεστι πράγματος λέγε .
sections.translation: Say what part you have in this matter .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: καὶ μαρτυρήσων ἦλθον — ἔστι γὰρ νόμῳ [*] [*]
sections.translation: I have come both to bear witness — for this man was a lawful suppliant and a guest of my sanctuary , and I am his purifier from bloodshed — and to be his advocate myself . I am

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: τῆς τοῦδε μητρὸς τοῦ [*] φόνου . σὺ δ εἴσαγε
sections.translation: responsible for the murder of his mother . To Athena . Bring in the case , and , in accordance with your wisdom , decide it .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: ὑμῶν ὁ μῦθος , εἰσάγω δὲ τὴν δίκην . [*]
sections.translation: To the Furies . It is for you to speak — I am only bringing in the case ; for the prosecutor at the beginning , speaking first , shall rightly inform us of the matter .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: πολλαὶ μέν ἐσμεν , λέξομεν δὲ συντόμως .
sections.translation: We are many , but we will speak briefly . To Orestes . Answer our questions , one by one . Say first if you killed your mother .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: ἔκτεινα : τούτου δ οὔτις ἄρνησις πέλει .
sections.translation: I killed her . There is no denial of this .

sections.label: Section 589
sections.original: ἓν μὲν τόδ ἤδη τῶν τριῶν παλαισμάτων .
sections.translation: Of the three falls that win the wrestling match , this one is already ours .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: οὐ κειμένῳ πω τόνδε κομπάζεις λόγον .
sections.translation: You make this boast over a man who is not down yet .

sections.label: Section 591
sections.original: εἰπεῖν γε μέντοι δεῖ σ ὅπως κατέκτανες .
sections.translation: You must , however , say how you killed her .

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: λέγω : ξιφουλκῷ χειρὶ πρὸς δέρην τεμών .
sections.translation: I will say it : with drawn sword in hand , I stabbed her in the throat .

sections.label: Section 593
sections.original: πρὸς τοῦ δ ἐπείσθης καὶ τίνος βουλεύμασι ;
sections.translation: By whom were you persuaded and on whose advice ?

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: τοῖς τοῦδε θεσφάτοισι : μαρτυρεῖ δέ μοι .
sections.translation: By the oracles of this god here ; he is my witness .

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ὁ μάντις ἐξηγεῖτό σοι μητροκτονεῖν ;
sections.translation: The prophet directed you to kill your mother ?

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: καὶ δεῦρό γ ἀεὶ τὴν τύχην οὐ μέμφομαι .
sections.translation: Yes , and to this very hour , I do not blame my fortune .

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: ἀλλ εἴ σε μάρψει ψῆφος , ἄλλ ἐρεῖς τάχα .
sections.translation: But if the jury ’ s vote catches hold of you , you ’ ll soon speak differently .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: πέποιθ , ἀρωγὰς δ ἐκ τάφου πέμψει [*] πατήρ .
sections.translation: I have good confidence . My father will send protection from his grave .

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: νεκροῖσί νυν πέπισθι [*] μητέρα κτανών .
sections.translation: Put your confidence in the dead now , after you have killed your mother !

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: δυοῖν γὰρ εἶχε προσβολὰς μιασμάτοιν . [*]
sections.translation: I do , for she was twice afflicted with pollution .

sections.label: Section 601
sections.original: πῶς δή ; δίδαξον τοὺς δικάζοντας τάδε .
sections.translation: How so ? Teach the judges this .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: ἀνδροκτονοῦσα πατέρ ἐμὸν κατέκτανε .
sections.translation: By murdering her husband , she killed my father .

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: τοιγὰρ [*] σὺ μὲν ζῇς , ἡ δ ἐλευθέρα φόνῳ . [*]
sections.translation: And so , although you are alive , she is free of pollution by her death . [*]

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: τί δ οὐκ ἐκείνην ζῶσαν ἤλαυνες φυγῇ ;
sections.translation: But why did you not drive her into exile , while she lived ?

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: οὐκ ἦν ὅμαιμος φωτὸς ὃν κατέκτανεν .
sections.translation: She was not related by blood to the man she killed .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ μητρὸς τῆς ἐμῆς ἐν αἵματι ;
sections.translation: Then am I my mother ’ s kin by blood ?

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: πῶς γάρ σ ἔθρεψεν ἐντός , ὦ μιαιφόνε ,
sections.translation: How else could she have nurtured you , murderer , beneath her belt ? Do you reject the nearest kinship , that of a mother ?

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: ἤδη σὺ μαρτύρησον . ἐξηγοῦ δέ μοι ,
sections.translation: Apollo , give your testimony now . Explain , on my behalf ,

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: Ἄπολλον , εἴ σφε σὺν δίκῃ κατέκτανον .
sections.translation: whether I was justified in killing her . For I do not deny that I did it , as it is done . But decide whether this bloodshed was , to your mind , just or not , so that I may inform the court .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: λέξω πρὸς ὑμᾶς τόνδ Ἀθηναίας μέγαν
sections.translation: I will speak justly before you , Athena ’ s great tribunal ,—

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: θεσμὸν δικαίως , μάντις ὢν δ [*] οὐ ψεύσομαι .
sections.translation:
since I am a prophet , I cannot lie . I have never yet , on my oracular throne , said anything about a man or woman or city that Zeus , the father of the Olympians , did not command me to say .
Learn how strong this plea of justice is ;

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: βουλῇ [*] πιφαύσκω δ ὔμμ [*] ἐπισπέσθαι πατρός .
sections.translation: and I tell you to obey the will of my father ; for an oath is not more powerful than Zeus . [*]

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: Ζεύς , ὡς λέγεις σύ , τόνδε χρησμὸν ὤπασε ,
sections.translation: Zeus , as you say , gave you this oracular command , to tell Orestes here to avenge his father ’ s murder but to take no account at all of the honor due his mother ?

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι ταὐτὸν ἄνδρα γενναῖον θανεῖν
sections.translation: Yes , for it is not the same thing — the murder of a noble man , honored by a god-given scepter , and his murder indeed by a woman , not by rushing arrows sped from afar , as if by an Amazon , but as you will hear , Pallas , and those

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ψήφῳ διαιρεῖν τοῦδε πράγματος πέρι .
sections.translation:
who are sitting to decide by vote in this matter .
She received him from the expedition , where he had for the most part won success beyond expectation , [*] in the judgment of those favorable to him ; then , as he was stepping from the bath , on its very edge , she threw a cloak like a tent over it ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: κόπτει πεδήσασ ἄνδρα δαιδάλῳ πέπλῳ .
sections.translation: fettered her husband in an embroidered robe , and cut him down . This was his death , as I have told it to you — the death of a man wholly majestic , commander of the fleet . As for that woman , I have described her in such a way as to whet the indignation of the people who have been appointed to decide this case .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: πατρὸς προτιμᾷ Ζεὺς μόρον τῷ σῷ λόγῳ :
sections.translation: Zeus gives greater honor to a father ’ s death , according to what you say ; yet he himself bound his aged father , Cronus . How does this not contradict what you say ? I call on you as witnesses turning to the judges to hear these things .

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: ὦ παντομισῆ κνώδαλα , στύγη θεῶν ,
sections.translation: Oh , monsters utterly loathed and detested by the gods !

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: πέδαι μὲν ἂν λυθεῖεν , [*] ἔστι τοῦδ ἄκος , [*]
sections.translation: Zeus could undo fetters , there is a remedy for that , and many means of release . But when the dust has drawn up the blood of a man , once he is dead , there is no return to life . For this , my father has made no magic spells ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: οὑμός , τὰ δ ἄλλα πάντ ἄνω τε [*] καὶ κάτω
sections.translation: although he arranges all other things , turning them up and down ; nor does his exercise of force cost him a breath .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: πῶς γὰρ τὸ φεύγειν τοῦδ ὑπερδικεῖς ὅρα :
sections.translation: See how you advocate acquittal for this man ! After he has poured out his mother ’ s blood on the ground , shall he then live in his father ’ s house in Argos ?

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ποίοισι βωμοῖς χρώμενος τοῖς δημίοις ;
sections.translation: Which of the public altars shall he use ? What purification rite of the brotherhoods [*] will receive him ?

sections.label: Section 657
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτο λέξω , καὶ μάθ ὡς ὀρθῶς ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: I will explain this , too , and see how correctly I will speak . The mother of what is called her child is not the parent , but the nurse of the newly-sown embryo . [*]

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: τίκτει δ ὁ θρῴσκων , ἡ δ ἅπερ ξένῳ ξένη
sections.translation: The one who mounts is the parent , whereas she , as a stranger for a stranger , preserves the young plant , if the god does not harm it . And I will show you proof of what I say : a father might exist without a mother . A witness is here at hand , the child of Olympian Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: οὐδ ἐν σκότοισι νηδύος τεθραμμένη ,
sections.translation:
who was not nursed in the darkness of a womb , and she is such a child as no goddess could give birth to .
For my part , Pallas , as in all other matters , as I know how , I will make your city and people great ; and I have sent this man as a suppliant to your sanctuary

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ὅπως γένοιτο πιστὸς ἐς τὸ πᾶν χρόνου
sections.translation: so that he may be faithful for all time , and that you , goddess , might win him and those to come after him as a new ally and so that these pledges of faith might remain always , for the later generations of these people to cherish .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: ἤδη κελεύω τούσδ ἀπὸ γνώμης φέρειν
sections.translation: Am I to assume that enough has been said ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ψῆφον δικαίαν , ὡς ἅλις λελεγμένων ;
sections.translation: and shall I now command these jurors to cast an honest vote according to their judgment ?

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: ἡμῖν μὲν ἤδη πᾶν τετόξευται βέλος .
sections.translation: For our part , every bolt is already shot . But I am waiting to hear how the trial will be decided .

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: τί γάρ ; πρὸς ὑμῶν πῶς τιθεῖσ ἄμομφος ὦ ;
sections.translation: Why not ? As for you , To Apollo and Orestes . how shall I arrange matters so that I will not be blamed by you ?

sections.label: Section 679
sections.original: ἠκούσαθ ὧν ἠκούσατ , ἐν δὲ καρδίᾳ
sections.translation: You have heard what you have heard ;

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ψῆφον φέροντες ὅρκον αἰδεῖσθε , [*] ξένοι .
sections.translation: and as you cast your ballots , keep the oath sacred in your hearts , friends .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: κλύοιτ ἂν ἤδη θεσμόν , Ἀττικὸς λεώς ,
sections.translation: Hear now my ordinance , people of Attica , as you judge the first trial for bloodshed . In the future , even as now , this court of judges will always exist for the people of Aegeus .

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: πάγον δ Ἄρειον [*] τόνδ , Ἀμαζόνων ἕδραν
sections.translation: And this Hill of Ares , the seat and camp of the Amazons , when they came with an army in resentment against Theseus , and in those days built up this new citadel with lofty towers to rival his , and sacrificed to Ares , from which this rock takes its name ,

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: πέτρα , πάγος τ Ἄρειος : ἐν δὲ τῷ σέβας
sections.translation: the Hill of Ares : [*] on this hill , the reverence of the citizens , and fear , its kinsman , will hold them back from doing wrong by day and night alike , so long as they themselves do not pollute the laws with evil streams ; if you stain clear water

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: λαμπρὸν μιαίνων οὔποθ εὑρήσεις ποτόν .
sections.translation:
with filth , you will never find a drink .
Neither anarchy nor tyranny — this I counsel my citizens to support and respect , and not to drive fear wholly out of the city . For who among mortals , if he fears nothing , is righteous ?
